abstract: Assess whether quantum reservoirs (with PCA-encoded inputs) can outperform classical ones and how entanglement changes feature mapping.

ai_review.improvements:
- Integrate real dataset loaders for MNIST/Fashion-MNIST/EMNIST/Kuzushiji/CIFAR-10 and rerun the same iter_1 protocol (same seeds, thresholds, and parity rules), then refresh claim-support wording only if statuses change.
- Regenerate `paper/figures/stage2_entanglement_phase_map.pdf` with a fully vector export path (avoid rasterized colorbar/artist elements) and recompile.
- Address the remaining Underfull box warnings in `paper/main.tex` with local line-break/spacing adjustments and verify clean compile logs.
ai_review.label: omegaXiv AI Reviewer
ai_review.questions:
- Empirical conclusions remain surrogate-data based; external validity to full benchmark loaders is not yet demonstrated.
- `paper/figures/stage2_entanglement_phase_map.pdf` contains a tiny embedded raster image strip (17x344) despite otherwise vector-style output; this is non-blocking but should be cleaned for strict archival vector compliance.
- `paper/main.log` reports minor Underfull box warnings (lines 199-204 and 432-433); not a scientific blocker but worth polishing before camera-ready export.
ai_review.strengths: Generated a complete conference-style manuscript (`main.tex`) with formal theorem/proof blocks, 3 main-text figures plus appendix diagnostics, and integrated iter_1 staged-validation evidence. Export bundle includes compiled PDF, bibliography (`references.bib`), equation-usage map, and updated cross-phase tracking artifacts.
ai_review.summary: The manuscript is scientifically coherent and mode-aligned for a hybrid simulation study: formal claims are proved, experiments include baselines/uncertainty/ablations tied to claims, bibliography style and page-budget evidence are valid, and no blocking hygiene or citation-fabrication defects were found. Approval is granted with warnings limited to empirical external-validity scope and minor presentation-quality cleanups.
ai_review.weaknesses:
- Empirical conclusions remain surrogate-data based; external validity to full benchmark loaders is not yet demonstrated.
- `paper/figures/stage2_entanglement_phase_map.pdf` contains a tiny embedded raster image strip (17x344) despite otherwise vector-style output; this is non-blocking but should be cleaned for strict archival vector compliance.
- `paper/main.log` reports minor Underfull box warnings (lines 199-204 and 432-433); not a scientific blocker but worth polishing before camera-ready export.

artifacts_summary: Generated a complete conference-style manuscript (`main.tex`) with formal theorem/proof blocks, 3 main-text figures plus appendix diagnostics, and integrated iter_1 staged-validation evidence. Export bundle includes compiled PDF, bibliography (`references.bib`), equation-usage map, and updated cross-phase tracking artifacts.
constraints: Target deadline provided: 2026-03-10 (already in the past relative to intake timestamp 2026-03-23)
evaluation_plan: Run comparative simulations between quantum and classical reservoirs using PCA-compressed image inputs; evaluate classification performance and representation behavior with and without entanglement; include MNIST and at least one more challenging image dataset to test whether observed gains persist beyond highly separable settings.
full_description: The user asks whether there is a quantum advantage in quantum reservoir computing for image classification when PCA components are encoded as reservoir inputs (e.g., angle encoding). The proposed setup includes a transverse Ising reservoir and measurement of a subset of Pauli operators, with explicit comparison of feature transformations with and without entanglement. The user flags that MNIST may be overly easy after PCA and asks whether alternative datasets would be better test cases. Attached references provide directly relevant context on QELM/QRC image classification pipelines, data encoding effects on expressivity, kernel interpretations of supervised quantum models, and measurement/readout optimization in QRC.
goals: Determine whether PCA-encoded quantum reservoirs outperform classical reservoirs for image classification
Quantify the role of entanglement in representation quality and classification performance
Characterize feature-space transformations induced by the reservoir with and without entanglement
Assess whether MNIST is an adequate benchmark under PCA preprocessing
Identify better-suited datasets if MNIST is too separable

original_problem.request_text:
Title: Quantum Reservoir Computing
Abstract: Assess whether quantum reservoirs (with PCA‑encoded inputs) can outperform classical ones and how entanglement changes feature mapping.
Full Description: I want to know if there is a quantum advantage in Quantum Reservoir Computing for image classification. If we take an image, and compute its PCA decomposition, and I feed the first X components to the reservoir as inputs (e.g. angle encoding), is there an advantage to using a quantum reservoir? What is the role of entanglement? How does the reservoir transform the features with and without entanglement? As a reservoir, we can take a simple transverse Ising model and angle encoding, and measuring a subset of pauli operators. You can try the MNIST dataset for digit classification, although I feel that the PCA is already so discriminatory (the only information in the picture is the digit, so *any* substantial difference between pictures immediately translates to a different classificaiton) that it will be hard to improve anything with QRC. Would other datasets be better test cases? 

Papers listing to download for review:

https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.06873
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2409.00998
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.14677
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2008.08605
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2101.11020
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2407.02553
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2412.06758
Problem Type: Open Question
Difficulty: Advanced
Domain: Quantum Computing
Originator: Marius-Constantin Dinu
Compute Budget: 1 APU, Apple Silicon, 128GB
Required Accelerator: CPU only available
Target Deadline: 2026-03-10
Tags: quantum computing, reservoir computing
Categories: Quantum Computing
Expected Artifacts: Paper, Code, Package
Related Links: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.06873, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2409.00998, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.14677, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2008.08605, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2101.11020
Dataset Links: None
